data.title: Sirius XM Appoints New Chief Legal Officer and Secretary
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sirius XM Holdings ( (SIRI) ) has shared an announcement.

On January 29, 2026, Sirius XM Holdings Inc. announced that Eve Konstan will become Executive Vice President, Chief Legal Officer and Secretary, effective February 9, 2026, succeeding long-time legal chief Richard N. Baer, who plans to retire in March 2026 after remaining in his current role until February 8 and then serving part-time through March 6 to facilitate a smooth transition without receiving severance. Konstan, a veteran media and entertainment lawyer with senior roles at Spotify, WarnerMedia Entertainment and HBO, joins under a multi-year employment agreement featuring a $1 million base salary, performance-linked bonus eligibility and a substantial mix of time- and performance-based restricted stock units tied in part to SiriusXM’s free cash flow and relative total shareholder return, underscoring the company’s emphasis on experienced legal leadership and incentive-aligned executive compensation as it advances strategic priorities and seeks to deliver long-term value for listeners, partners and shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (SIRI) stock is a Hold with a $21.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sirius XM Holdings stock, see the SIRI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SIRI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SIRI is a Neutral.

Sirius XM Holdings’ overall stock score reflects a solid valuation and strong cash flow management, which are offset by challenges in revenue growth and technical indicators suggesting potential resistance. The earnings call highlights strategic initiatives that could drive future growth, but current subscriber metrics remain a concern.

To see Spark’s full report on SIRI stock, click here.

More about Sirius XM Holdings

Sirius XM Holdings Inc. is the leading audio entertainment company in North America, operating a portfolio that includes its flagship subscription satellite and online radio service SiriusXM, Pandora’s ad-supported and premium music streaming offerings, a broad podcast network, and related advertising and business solutions, reaching about 170 million listeners monthly with music, talk, news, and sports programming.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Allarity Therapeutics ( (ALLR) ).

On January 28, 2026, Allarity Therapeutics, Inc. entered into a Common Stock Purchase Agreement with Tumim Stone Capital LLC, giving the company the right, but not the obligation, to sell up to $6 million of newly issued common shares, or a lower amount if capped by exchange rules. The shares may be sold in tranches at discounts to specified volume-weighted average prices and are subject to limits on trade size, ownership thresholds for the investor, and an exchange cap that restricts issuance to 19.99% of pre-agreement outstanding shares unless shareholders later approve a waiver or the minimum price condition is met; Allarity paid a $45,000 commitment fee for the facility, which provides a flexible source of equity financing but also introduces potential dilution for existing shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (ALLR) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Allarity Therapeutics stock, see the ALLR Stock Forecast page.

Arrow Financial ( (AROW) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 28, 2026, Arrow Financial said long-serving director Elizabeth A. Miller will retire from its board after the 2026 annual meeting, ending a tenure that began in 2017. The company also reported record fourth-quarter 2025 net income of $14.0 million, or $0.85 per share, up sharply from $4.5 million and $0.27 a year earlier, and full-year 2025 earnings of $44.0 million, or $2.65 per share, compared with $29.7 million and $1.77 in 2024, driven by strong net interest margin expansion, higher net interest income and improved asset yields alongside lower funding costs. Return on average assets reached 1.24% for the quarter and 1.00% for the year, tangible book value per share grew more than 10% in 2025, and credit quality remained solid with low net charge-offs, underscoring the operational benefits of its recent system integration and strategic execution. Reflecting the stronger performance and signaling confidence in future earnings capacity, the board on January 28, 2026 declared a first-quarter 2026 cash dividend of $0.30 per share, payable on February 25, 2026 to shareholders of record on February 11, 2026, a 3.4% increase from the prior quarterly payout.

The most recent analyst rating on (AROW) stock is a Buy with a $35.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arrow Financial stock, see the AROW Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Nasdaq Reports Strong Q4 and Full-Year 2025 Results
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nasdaq ( (NDAQ) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 29, 2026, Nasdaq reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, with net revenue reaching $5.25 billion, up 13% year over year, and Solutions revenue surpassing $4.0 billion, up 12%. Fourth-quarter net revenue rose 13% to $1.39 billion, driven by 13% growth in Solutions revenue, 16% growth in Market Services net revenue, and particularly robust performance in Financial Technology, where revenue climbed 14%, and Index revenue, which jumped 23% on the back of $99 billion in net inflows over the past twelve months. Annualized recurring revenue reached $3.05 billion, up 10%, with SaaS revenue representing 38% of ARR, underscoring the company’s shift toward recurring, technology-led income streams. Profitability improved markedly, with GAAP diluted EPS up 60% for the year and 47% in the quarter, and non-GAAP diluted EPS rising 24% for 2025 and 27% in the fourth quarter, while operating leverage benefited from only modest increases in operating expenses despite higher investment in technology and talent. Nasdaq also continued to return capital to shareholders in the fourth quarter of 2025, distributing $153 million in dividends and executing $286 million in share repurchases while repaying $100 million of senior unsecured notes, reinforcing management’s confidence in the durability of the business model and the strength of its cash generation.

The most recent analyst rating on (NDAQ) stock is a Buy with a $113.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nasdaq stock, see the NDAQ Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NDAQ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NDAQ is a Outperform.

The main constraint is valuation (high P/E with a modest dividend), while the latest earnings call supports the outlook with record solutions revenue and rising ARR, tempered by slower IPO timing and some product-line execution risks.

To see Spark’s full report on NDAQ stock, click here.

Nasdaq, Inc. is a global financial services and technology company best known for operating the Nasdaq stock exchange and providing market services, index licensing, and a broad suite of technology and data-driven solutions for capital markets and financial institutions. Its business is increasingly focused on high-growth “Solutions” offerings, including financial technology platforms, index products, and software-as-a-service tools that generate recurring revenue from institutional and corporate clients worldwide.

data.title: Gentherm to Merge with Modine Performance Technologies Unit
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gentherm ( (THRM) ) has provided an announcement.

On January 29, 2026, Gentherm and Modine announced a definitive agreement to spin off and simultaneously combine Modine’s Performance Technologies business with Gentherm in a Reverse Morris Trust transaction valued at about $1.0 billion, creating a scaled leader in thermal management solutions with pro forma revenue of $2.6 billion, a 13% synergy-adjusted EBITDA margin and net leverage of roughly 1.0x. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, will leave Modine shareholders owning about 40% and Gentherm shareholders about 60% of the combined company, while Modine retains its Climate Solutions unit as a pure-play data center and commercial HVAC and refrigeration business; the transaction is expected to broaden Gentherm’s portfolio, reduce its dependence on light vehicles, unlock cost and commercial synergies including cross-selling and new market access, and give Modine investors exposure to two more focused, higher-growth thermal and climate platforms.

An announcement from Coastal Financial ( (CCB) ) is now available.

On January 29, 2026, Coastal Financial Corporation released investor presentation materials detailing its financial and operational performance for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, highlighted by continued loan and deposit growth, technology investments, and expansion of its BaaS-driven CCBX platform. In the fourth quarter of 2025, total loans receivable rose 1.2% to $3.75 billion and deposits increased 4.3% to $4.14 billion, while core net revenue grew 6.8% despite a 4.6% decline in total revenue tied to lower credit enhancement revenue and higher noninterest expense that pressured diluted EPS to $0.82 and ROA to 1.09%. The company sold $2.98 billion of loans, swept $843.6 million of deposits off balance sheet for insurance and liquidity income, and acquired the GreenFi climate-focused brand to strengthen its strategic optionality. Coastal also reported strong growth in its CCBX franchise, with deposits in that segment up $182.9 million, off-balance-sheet credit card accounts climbing 39% quarter over quarter to 550,977, and fee-earning CCBX debit cards increasing to 8.7 million, while advancing multiple artificial intelligence initiatives and outlining a defined AI roadmap to improve efficiency, risk management, and customer experience and to support future digital asset and digital deposit offerings.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stellar Bancorp ( (STEL) ) has shared an announcement.

On January 27, 2026, Stellar Bancorp, Inc. agreed to merge with Prosperity Bancshares, Inc. in an all-stock and cash transaction under which Stellar will be combined into Prosperity, followed immediately by the merger of Stellar Bank into Prosperity Bank, leaving Prosperity and its banking subsidiary as the surviving entities. Stellar shareholders are to receive 0.3803 shares of Prosperity common stock plus $11.36 in cash for each Stellar share, with detailed treatment laid out for stock options, restricted stock and performance unit awards, and the combined organization’s governance structure expanded to add two Stellar-affiliated directors to the boards of Prosperity and Prosperity Bank. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, is subject to customary shareholder, regulatory and stock exchange approvals as well as tax opinions, includes a $78 million termination fee payable by Stellar under certain conditions, and is supported by voting and director support agreements with Stellar directors who control about 8.8% of Stellar’s shares and have agreed to vote in favor of the transaction and observe post-closing non-solicitation and confidentiality restrictions.

The most recent analyst rating on (STEL) stock is a Buy with a $37.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Stellar Bancorp stock, see the STEL Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

Bread Financial Holdings ( (BFH) ) has provided an update.

On January 29, 2026, Bread Financial reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results showing solid profitability and capital return alongside a stronger balance sheet. For 2025, the company generated $518 million in net income available to common stockholders, nearly doubled earnings per diluted share year over year, and lifted tangible book value per common share by 23% to $57.57, supported by higher net interest income, improved credit performance and lower credit-loss provisions. Management highlighted a resilient consumer backdrop, modest 3% growth in credit sales and stable partner relationships, with all top 10 programs renewed into at least 2028, while direct-to-consumer deposits rose 11% to $8.5 billion, now nearly half of total funding. The company also optimized its capital structure by retiring high-cost debt, issuing subordinated debt and preferred equity, and repurchasing $350 million of common stock in 2025, actions that contributed to credit rating upgrades and an improved outlook from major agencies. In conjunction with the earnings release, the board declared quarterly cash dividends payable on March 16, 2026 of $26.35 per share on its Series A preferred stock and $0.23 per share on common stock to shareholders of record on February 27, 2026, signaling continued commitment to shareholder returns while maintaining a common equity Tier 1 capital ratio of 13.0%.

The most recent analyst rating on (BFH) stock is a Sell with a $62.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bread Financial Holdings stock, see the BFH Stock Forecast page.

Westwater Resources ( (WWR) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 29, 2026, Westwater Resources’ Executive Chairman Terence Cryan was scheduled to present at the DealFlow Discovery Conference, using an updated investor presentation that outlines progress and plans for the company’s graphite business. The materials highlight that the Kellyton Graphite Plant is under construction with more than half of its $245 million capital cost already invested, Phase 1 capacity of 12,500 metric tons per year of battery-grade natural graphite nearly sold out under two offtake agreements, and plans to expand total capacity to 50,000 metric tons per year, supported by a qualification line and a patented purification process that has helped secure customers. The presentation also underscores the strategic significance of Westwater’s Coosa Graphite Deposit—covering about 42,000 acres, with drilling to date indicating 2.3 million short tons of natural graphite and a 2023 assessment pointing to a 22-year mine life at 99,000 short tons per year—reinforcing the company’s domestic, first-mover advantage as graphite demand accelerates with the growth of electric vehicles and energy storage.

More about Westwater Resources

Westwater Resources, listed on the NYSE American under the symbol WWR, is developing a vertically integrated graphite business in the United States, focused on producing battery-grade natural graphite for lithium-ion battery anodes. The company’s operations center on its Kellyton Graphite Plant in Kellyton, Alabama, and its Coosa Graphite Deposit in Coosa County, Alabama—one of the largest graphite deposits in the contiguous U.S.—positioning it as a potential first U.S.-based producer of battery-ready graphite serving electric vehicle, energy storage and national defense markets.

data.description:

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

Significant shareholder Chidozie Ugwumba has recently reduced his position in Clene through two notable stock sales. In the first transaction, he sold 10,557 shares of Clene stock for a total value of $53,244. In a second transaction, he disposed of an additional 8,717 shares, generating proceeds of $42,800. Together, these back-to-back sales highlight a meaningful shift in Ugwumba’s holdings in the company.

Recent Updates on CLNN stock

In the last 24 hours, Clene’s stock has been driven by two main developments: a sizeable, milestone-linked financing and progress on its ALS program with the FDA. The company entered into a registered direct offering of over $28 million in three tranches, with an initial ~$6 million equity raise and Series A/B warrants whose exercise prices step up if CNM‑Au8 achieves key FDA milestones and if the share price trades above specified levels. Analysts’ recent stance and target assumptions are being influenced by this structure because it both extends Clene’s cash runway into at least the third quarter of 2026 and potentially through late 2026/early 2027, while also signaling future dilution and embedding expectations for successful NDA filing and approval. At the same time, the FDA granted Clene an in‑person Type C meeting this quarter to review biomarker data supporting a potential ALS NDA, including statistically significant reductions in NfL and GFAP and exploratory HEALEY trial findings linking CNM‑Au8 and IGFBP7 biomarker declines to reduced mortality risk. These regulatory and clinical signals are a key basis for analysts’ revised assumptions on Clene’s risk‑reward profile—supporting longer‑term opportunity in ALS—while their caution is grounded in the company’s weak current financials, ongoing cash burn, and bearish technicals, which temper expectations despite the biomarker and regulatory momentum.

Spark’s Take on CLNN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CLNN is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (minimal and declining revenue, large ongoing losses, negative equity and continued cash burn). Technicals also reflect a bearish trend with weak momentum. Offsetting factors include positive corporate developments (financing that extends runway and FDA engagement around biomarker-driven regulatory strategy), but they do not yet resolve the underlying financial risk.

data.title: Contrasting Insider Moves Rock Live Oak Bancshares Leadership Roster
data.description:
New insider activity at Live Oak Bancshares ( (LOB) ) has taken place on January 28, 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Live Oak Bancshares has seen notable insider activity, with Chief Experience Officer Courtney Spencer selling 9,700 shares of company stock in a transaction valued at $378,300, while Chief Risk Officer Ewa Maria Stasiowska moved in the opposite direction by purchasing 2,595 shares worth $99,985. These contrasting trades, involving hundreds of thousands of dollars in total, highlight differing insider positions within the company’s leadership ranks.

Recent Updates on LOB stock

Over the last day, sentiment around Live Oak Bancshares has been driven mainly by its stronger‑than‑expected Q4 results and upbeat outlook, which led at least one analyst (Keefe Bruyette) to lift its valuation expectations for the stock. The earnings call highlighted record loan production, double‑digit loan and revenue growth, and a sharp jump in profitability and EPS driven by operating leverage, as well as resilient net interest income and margin despite recent Fed cuts. Management also emphasized rapid progress in building low‑cost deposits and business checking relationships, growing cross‑sell (more customers holding both loans and deposits), and the scaling of its Live Oak Express SBA platform, all of which support a structurally better funding mix and fee income profile. While there are acknowledged headwinds—including rising nonaccrual loans in the SBA portfolio, near‑term margin pressure from timing mismatches in loan and deposit repricing after rate cuts, and some one‑time expense items—credit trends are described as stabilizing and comparatively strong versus peers. The combination of record growth, margin resilience, a more deposit‑rich balance sheet, and management’s guidance for continued expansion in net interest income and manageable credit costs appears to be the key rationale behind recent positive adjustments to analysts’ expectations, even as investors remain mindful of short‑term credit and margin risks.

Spark’s Take on LOB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LOB is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid fundamentals with a strong balance sheet but weaker cash flow and profitability trends. Technicals add support with a clear uptrend and positive momentum, while valuation is only average due to a higher P/E and low yield. The latest earnings call was constructive on growth and longer-term margin trajectory, tempered by near-term NIM compression and elevated nonaccruals.
